atoms.source_snippet: pub struct SpcVerifierImpl<N> where N: FullNodeTypes { inner: Arc<ConcreteSpcVerifier<N::Provider>>, _phantom: PhantomData<N> } ... pub fn new(...) -> Self
atoms.semantic_claim: The Ethereum node crate defines a generic SpcVerifierImpl<N> bridge that stores a provider-backed ritual_spc_verification verifier and exposes a constructor for nodebuilder wiring.

destinations.evidence: The bridge now converts trait requests into concrete verification requests, delegates batch/optimized verification, exposes previous-block lookup, no-ops ensure_registry_synced, and forwards registry updates.

destinations.snippet: pub trait SpcVerifier: Send + Sync { fn verify_batch(...); fn verify_parallel_optimized(...); fn get_previous_block_data(...); fn ensure_registry_synced(...); fn update_registry_from_data(...); }
destinations.evidence: The EVM trait surface required by nodebuilder is present in the Reth port.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Compile ritual-reth-nodebuilder-internal against the v2.2.0 port and expect unresolved reth_node_ethereum::SpcVerifierImpl if the bridge remains absent.
atoms.notes: Resolved by 7f8a668d24: crates/ethereum/node/src/spc.rs now implements this SpcVerifierImpl bridge behavior.

atoms.source_snippet: fn verify_batch(&self, requests: Vec<SpcVerificationRequest>, max_parallelism: usize) -> ...
atoms.semantic_claim: verify_batch converts trait-level SPC requests into concrete verifier requests while preserving input/output/proof/block/transaction context, executor, precompile address, previous-block fields, proposer signature, error/success, and max_parallelism, then maps batch results back into the trait result shape.

destinations.evidence: The bridge now converts trait requests into concrete verification requests, delegates batch/optimized verification, exposes previous-block lookup, no-ops ensure_registry_synced, and forwards registry updates.

destinations.snippet: pub trait SpcVerifier: Send + Sync { fn verify_batch(...); fn verify_parallel_optimized(...); fn get_previous_block_data(...); fn ensure_registry_synced(...); fn update_registry_from_data(...); }
destinations.evidence: The EVM trait surface required by nodebuilder is present in the Reth port.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Create multiple SPC verification requests with distinct executor, previous-block, proposer-signature, success/error, and precompile fields; assert a destination bridge forwards every field and preserves per-result errors, counts, and duration.
atoms.notes: Resolved by 7f8a668d24: crates/ethereum/node/src/spc.rs now implements this SpcVerifierImpl bridge behavior.

atoms.semantic_claim: verify_parallel_optimized performs the same full field-preserving request conversion as verify_batch, delegates to the concrete optimized parallel verifier, and maps concrete batch results into trait-level success/failure results and duration.

destinations.evidence: The bridge now converts trait requests into concrete verification requests, delegates batch/optimized verification, exposes previous-block lookup, no-ops ensure_registry_synced, and forwards registry updates.

destinations.snippet: pub trait SpcVerifier: Send + Sync { fn verify_batch(...); fn verify_parallel_optimized(...); fn get_previous_block_data(...); fn ensure_registry_synced(...); fn update_registry_from_data(...); }
destinations.evidence: The EVM trait surface required by nodebuilder is present in the Reth port.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Exercise optimized SPC verification with one valid and one invalid request and assert ordering, per-item errors, counts, and duration propagation.
atoms.notes: Resolved by 7f8a668d24: crates/ethereum/node/src/spc.rs now implements this SpcVerifierImpl bridge behavior.

atoms.source_snippet: fn get_previous_block_data(&self, block_number: u64) -> Result<(B256, u64, u64), Self::Error>
atoms.semantic_claim: The bridge exposes previous-block hash, number, and timestamp lookup through the trait by delegating to the concrete provider-backed verifier and normalizing lookup failures into SpcVerificationError.

destinations.evidence: The bridge now converts trait requests into concrete verification requests, delegates batch/optimized verification, exposes previous-block lookup, no-ops ensure_registry_synced, and forwards registry updates.

destinations.snippet: pub trait SpcVerifier: Send + Sync { fn verify_batch(...); fn verify_parallel_optimized(...); fn get_previous_block_data(...); fn ensure_registry_synced(...); fn update_registry_from_data(...); }
destinations.evidence: The EVM trait surface required by nodebuilder is present in the Reth port.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Request previous-block data for block N through the destination trait object and assert N-1 hash, number, and timestamp are returned, with fail-closed behavior when unavailable.
atoms.notes: Resolved by 7f8a668d24: crates/ethereum/node/src/spc.rs now implements this SpcVerifierImpl bridge behavior.

atoms.semantic_claim: ensure_registry_synced deliberately satisfies the SPC verifier trait with a no-op success future, relying on execution-time registry updates rather than lazy registry syncing before verification.

destinations.evidence: The bridge now converts trait requests into concrete verification requests, delegates batch/optimized verification, exposes previous-block lookup, no-ops ensure_registry_synced, and forwards registry updates.

destinations.snippet: pub trait SpcVerifier: Send + Sync { fn verify_batch(...); fn verify_parallel_optimized(...); fn get_previous_block_data(...); fn ensure_registry_synced(...); fn update_registry_from_data(...); }
destinations.evidence: The EVM trait surface required by nodebuilder is present in the Reth port.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Call ensure_registry_synced on the destination SPC verifier trait object and assert it returns Ok(()) without external sync attempts, with freshness supplied by execution-time update_registry_from_data.
atoms.notes: Resolved by 7f8a668d24: crates/ethereum/node/src/spc.rs now implements this SpcVerifierImpl bridge behavior.

atoms.source_snippet: fn update_registry_from_data(&self, executors: Vec<(Address, Address, u8, Vec<u8>, FixedBytes<32>, u8)>, block_number: u64) -> Result<(), Self::Error>
atoms.semantic_claim: update_registry_from_data forwards execution-state executor registry tuples, including payment address, TEE address, TEE type, public key, workload ID, capability byte, and block number, into the concrete SPC verifier registry update path and maps update failures into the trait error type.

destinations.evidence: The bridge now converts trait requests into concrete verification requests, delegates batch/optimized verification, exposes previous-block lookup, no-ops ensure_registry_synced, and forwards registry updates.

destinations.snippet: pub trait SpcVerifier: Send + Sync { fn verify_batch(...); fn verify_parallel_optimized(...); fn get_previous_block_data(...); fn ensure_registry_synced(...); fn update_registry_from_data(...); }
destinations.evidence: The EVM trait surface required by nodebuilder is present in the Reth port.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: After a block reads two TEE registry executors with different capability bytes and workload IDs, verify the destination SPC verifier registry is updated and later SPC verification uses the forwarded public key/capability data.
atoms.notes: Resolved by 7f8a668d24: crates/ethereum/node/src/spc.rs now implements this SpcVerifierImpl bridge behavior.
